marketState: neutral-to-bearish — storage overhang and a bearish-tilted seasonal window offset by a hot near-term weather bounce
summary: Front NG sits at $3.01, essentially at fair value ($2.92 now, band $2.42-$3.42) with no strong mean-reversion edge either way. The medium-term picture skews bearish: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average after a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, the 8-16 day weather window has faded back to near-normal (rangePos 0.41), and the current seasonal calendar point historically favors lower prices over the next 4 weeks (only 31% odds higher, typical -3.1% move), though that seasonality read is flagged 'Low' reliability. Daily/weekly technicals confirm a bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA on every timeframe), but 1h/4h RSI is oversold and price sits below the lower daily Bollinger band, raising the odds of a short-term bounce fed by unusually hot 1-7 day weather demand (+15.9% vs 5y, near the top of its range). Managed money is net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, so the position isn't crowded enough to force a squeeze. Net: lean short into strength on the longer horizon, but respect near-term bounce risk from weather and oversold momentum.

longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus is building against a 5y-above-normal base with a bearish supply surprise, the 8-16D weather window has faded to near-normal, and this point in the seasonal calendar has historically favored lower prices over the next month (31% odds higher, -3.1% typical move). Price is already near fair value with a wide $1 band, so there's no fair-value tailwind either direction, and managed-money shorts aren't crowded (31st percentile), leaving room for the position to build further.
longTerm.invalidation: A bullish storage surprise (smaller-than-forecast build) or a sustained heat wave that pushes 8-16D demand materially above the 5y range would break the bearish thesis; a weekly close above $3.45 (fair-value ceiling) invalidates the trade.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7 day weather demand is running hot (+15.9% vs 5y, rangePos 0.92 near the top of its range), and momentum is stretched to the downside (1h RSI 27, 4h RSI 23.4, daily price below the lower Bollinger band), favoring a short-term mean-reversion bounce. This is explicitly countertrend to the bearish daily/weekly EMA structure (50<200 on every timeframe), so it's a tactical, small-size trade rather than a trend follow.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and close below $2.87 signals the oversold bounce failed and the broader bearish trend is reasserting; fading 1-7D weather demand back toward normal would also remove the catalyst.
